$comment: Windy Search ops surface — Class C (cloud), replicates the Mind pattern. The PRODUCT API (/v1/search, /v1/fetch, /v1/extract) is Search's reason to exist and stays OUT of this contract per §2; this is the ops/healing + agent-self-diagnostic surface on https://api.windysearch.com. Reads bound via transport. Search is EPT-gated end to end, so even the self-diagnostics carry the caller's passport. Rev 2026-07-13 (gap-closing, replicating windy-mind #60): get_logs + get_config + run_selftest SHIPPED as the /ops/* trio (EPT-required). get_logs is content-free BY CONSTRUCTION (fixed event vocabulary; bridge failures categorized — error bodies can echo query text, never stored raw). run_selftest runs ONE real canary query through the router's normal path (all configured sources, merge, dedup) and caches its verdict 300s — Brave/Google are METERED, so polling must not drain a bridge budget; payload uses `passed`, never top-level `ok` (invoke-envelope rule). Remaining gaps (set_setting, apply_update, restart_app): the windy-mind ops-hook pattern (windy-mind #61) generalizes — port it per-host when Search's turn comes. DEPLOY STATUS: merged; live after the Grant-gated deploy — probe before trusting mid-incident.

get_capabilities.note: reports configured search sources — a search service with zero bridges is degraded (found live 2026-07-06); this IS the capability probe.

get_config.note: per-source configured/fallback booleans + redis/integrity/telemetry/render wiring, secrets fully redacted — closed 2026-07-13.

get_logs.note: content-free ring, fixed vocabulary; bridge failures categorized — closed 2026-07-13.

run_selftest.note: one real canary query via the router's normal path, counts-only verdict, 300s cache (metered bridges) — closed 2026-07-13.

reconnect.note: stateless cloud service; nothing to reconnect.

restart_app.note: out-of-band; port the windy-mind ops-hook (windy-mind #61 — out-of-patient systemd unit, bearer + confirm nonce) to this host.

enter_safe_mode.note: stateless; degraded-mode is reported by /health/ready, not toggled.

set_setting.note: no runtime-config mutation; source keys are env-at-deploy.

check_for_update.note: resolves the running version against admin's /v1/fleet-versions; status + literal remediation (apply_update). Closed 2026-07-13.

apply_update.note: SSH-free health-gated redeploy; §7. The windy-mind ops-hook (#61: rebuild + last-good rollback + sha attestation) is the pattern to port to this host.

reset_to_defaults.note: stateless service — no user state to reset.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST call when Windy Search seems down. Liveness of the search service: returns ok + service + version + environment if the process is up.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Read Windy Search's deployment identity — which build is serving right now. Use this to check whether a fix that was meant to deploy is actually live.

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Report whether Windy Search is actually READY to serve — is Redis reachable, and how many search sources are wired up. A search service with zero configured sources returns empty results while looking 'alive'; this is the honest capability probe that catches that.

tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Show the passport Windy Search sees for the calling agent — the parsed EPT claims. Use this to diagnose 'it says I'm not who I am' or auth confusion.

tools.name: get_integrity_budget
tools.description: Show the calling agent's current integrity (EII) tier and its remaining rate-limit budget at Windy Search. Use this to answer 'why am I being throttled' or 'how many more searches can I do right now'.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.description: Read Windy Search's recent operational events — server starts, per-source bridge failures by category (quota exhausted, auth/billing, upstream errors), and self-test results. Content-free by construction: the log can only ever hold fixed event names and category codes, never a query, a result, or a raw bridge error. Requires your passport.

entries.description: [{ts, level, event, code?}] — fixed vocabulary only

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: Read Windy Search's effective runtime configuration with every secret redacted to a boolean: which search sources actually hold keys (and which are fallbacks), whether Redis, Eternitas integrity-reporting, telemetry, and the page-render chain are wired. Use this when 'search returns nothing' — a service with zero configured sources looks alive but is empty. Requires your passport.

tools.name: run_selftest
tools.description: Actively exercise Windy Search's real core path: one canary query routed through every configured source exactly the way an agent's search would be (fan-out, merge, dedup). Reports pass/fail per stage with result COUNTS, which bridges answered, and latency — never the results themselves. The verdict is cached for 5 minutes because search bridges are metered: polling this cannot drain a bridge budget. Requires your passport.

passed.description: true only when every stage passed ('passed', not 'ok' — top-level ok is reserved for the invoke envelope)

stages.description: [{name: config|search, ok, detail}] — counts/bridges/latency, never result content

tools.name: check_for_update
tools.description: Check whether a newer Windy Search build is available. Resolves the running version against the fleet's version manifest and reports current, update-available (the notes say what it fixes), or must-update (below the minimum supported version). Hands back the literal next step when an update exists. Read-only — never deploys; applying is the separate always-confirm apply_update.
